ops.title: cc-guides pack changes propagate via each repo's daily cron only — never run a manual whole-fleet render sweep or dispatch loop

ops.body:
On 2026-07-14 a Models-table pack change was propagated by a 45-agent manual render sweep
across ~37 repos. The user's correction: the sweep should never have happened — "each member
of the fleet should have a daily job to pull and re-render from the latest fragments" — and a
pack change that lands after today's render window simply rides tomorrow's cron (fleet-wide
within 24h). Claude "should have just been ok leaving it to the next 24h job."

**Why:** the latency win is at most ~22h and almost never matters, while the sweep cost ~2M
tokens, an hour of orchestration, and real risk on shared working copies (dirty trees, a
divergent-bookmark incident). The pull model was audited healthy the same day: 43/43 consumer
repos have the canonical guides.yml stub byte-identical, workflow state `active`, and green
scheduled runs.

**How to apply:** after pushing a cc-guides pack change (cc-skills `plugin/guides/**`), stop —
the fleet self-updates. (Since the v4 CI-owned redesign of 2026-07-21, active repos also pull
the latest pack on their own next push, so effective latency is usually well under the cron's
24h; the rule is unchanged.) Never run a render sweep or a fleet `repos/*/dispatches` loop. The
enablers were removed 2026-07-14: the guides-lint.yml fleet-dispatch runbook comment (now
states the pull-only rule, cc-skills c4f04c39) and the orphaned FLEET_DISPATCH_TOKEN secret
on cc-skills (PAT revocation flagged to the user). A single repo that genuinely needs its
render early: `gh workflow run guides.yml -R yasyf/<repo>` — one repo, never a loop.
Reliability caveat: GitHub disables crons after 60 days of repo inactivity (scheduled runs
don't count as activity); re-enable with `gh workflow enable`. Related:
[[model-routing-convention]] (the v5 rollout whose sweep triggered this),
[[cc-guides-v3-architecture]].
